edges.description: Variants in genes encoding basal body, axonemal, and intraflagellar transport components compromise primary cilium biogenesis.
edges.target: Ciliary gene mutations disrupt primary cilium assembly

edges.description: Variants in transition zone genes (e.g., TMEM67, CC2D2A, MKS1, CEP290, B9D2) disrupt the ciliary gate.
Variants in ciliary tip module genes (CEP104, CSPP1, TOGARAM1, ARMC9, CCDC66) disrupt axonemal microtubule growth.
Loss of AHI1/Jouberin (and CEP290) disrupts cilium-coupled canonical Wnt signaling at the cerebellar dorsal midline.
edges.target: Defective canonical Wnt signaling at the cerebellar midline

edges.description: Failed cilium assembly produces cilia with abnormal number, morphology, and length.
edges.source: Ciliary gene mutations disrupt primary cilium assembly

edges.description: Defective primary cilium biogenesis disrupts the cilium machinery required for intraciliary transport.
edges.source: Ciliary gene mutations disrupt primary cilium assembly

edges.description: Loss of basal-body proteins (e.g., KIAA0586/Talpid3) required for cilium assembly disrupts hippocampal progenitor proliferation and migration.
edges.source: Ciliary gene mutations disrupt primary cilium assembly

edges.description: Abnormal cilium structure compromises the cilium-dependent transduction of Hedgehog signaling.
Photoreceptor outer segments are modified primary cilia and are particularly sensitive to abnormal cilium structure.
Renal tubular epithelial cilia mediate cilium-dependent homeostasis that is disrupted by abnormal cilium morphology.
Defective intraciliary cargo transport mislocalizes outer-segment proteins required for photoreceptor function.
Impaired ciliary transport disrupts renal tubular cilium-dependent signaling.
Disrupted ciliary trafficking impairs cilium-dependent neurodevelopmental signaling.
Disrupted ciliary signaling perturbs mid-hindbrain morphogenesis underlying the JS brain malformation.
Impaired neurodevelopmental programs lead to vermian hypoplasia, the cardinal cerebellar feature of JS.
Neurodevelopmental disruption manifests as intellectual disability.
Neurodevelopmental disruption manifests as global developmental delay.
Disrupted ciliary tip microtubule dynamics produce cilia of abnormal length and morphology.
A defective TZ gate compromises selective entry and exit of ciliary cargo.
TZ gating is required for ciliary localization of Hedgehog pathway components (SMO, GPR161, TULP3).
B9D2 also functions extra-ciliarly in tight junctions; its loss disrupts biliary epithelial polarity.
edges.target: Biliary ductal plate malformation via ciliary tight junction defect

edges.description: Reduced cilium-dependent Hedgehog signaling disrupts mid-hindbrain morphogenesis.
Cilium-dependent Hedgehog signaling is required for limb-bud patterning; its disruption causes preaxial/postaxial digit duplication.
Reduced Wnt-dependent proliferation at the dorsal midline produces vermis hypoplasia.
edges.source: Defective canonical Wnt signaling at the cerebellar midline

edges.description: Vermis hypoplasia is the dominant component of the molar tooth sign.
edges.source: Defective canonical Wnt signaling at the cerebellar midline

edges.description: Mid-hindbrain patterning defects underlie the failure of axonal decussation across the brainstem midline.
Failed midline morphogenesis produces vermian hypoplasia.
Mid-hindbrain malformation disrupts brainstem nuclei controlling respiratory rhythm and ocular motor pathways.
Elongated, non-decussating superior cerebellar peduncles plus deep interpeduncular fossa produce the molar tooth radiographic sign.
Disrupted brainstem motor pathways contribute to early hypotonia.
Loss of cerebellar output decussation contributes to ataxia.
Hippocampal neurogenesis defects contribute to learning/memory impairment.
Hippocampal and other ciliary neurogenesis defects manifest as global developmental delay.
Outer segment dystrophy and visual pigment mislocalization produce progressive retinal dystrophy.
Tubular ciliopathy drives the nephronophthisis-type tubulointerstitial kidney disease in JS.
Disrupted cilium-dependent tubular signaling produces renal cysts.
Ductal plate malformation drives congenital hepatic fibrosis.
edges.source: Biliary ductal plate malformation via ciliary tight junction defect

edges.description: Progressive hepatic fibrosis raises portal venous pressure.
edges.source: Biliary ductal plate malformation via ciliary tight junction defect

edges.description: Portal hypertension produces porto-systemic collaterals including esophageal varices.
edges.source: Biliary ductal plate malformation via ciliary tight junction defect

edges.description: Brainstem respiratory control dysfunction drives episodic tachypnea.
Brainstem respiratory control dysfunction drives apneic episodes.
Persistent brainstem control dysfunction contributes to sleep apnea later in life.
Brainstem ocular motor pathway disruption contributes to oculomotor apraxia.
Brainstem ocular motor pathway disruption produces abnormal eye movements.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlates in Joubert syndrome: A review.
evidence.snippet: On the other hand, individuals with causal variants in the CEP290 or AHI1 need a closer surveillance for retinal dystrophy and, in case of CEP290, also for chronic kidney disease.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome diagnosed renally late.
evidence.snippet: Variants in AHI1 account for ∼7% of Joubert syndrome cases and are associated either with ‘pure’ Joubert syndrome, or Joubert syndrome plus renal and/or retinal disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic spectrum from a prototype of ciliopathy: Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most common variants were in the CPLANE1, CEP290, and TMEM67 genes, and other causative genes were AHI1, ARMC9, CEP41, CSPP1, HYLS1, KATNIP, KIAA0586, KIF7, RPGRIP1L, including some previously unreported variants in these genes.

nodes.description: Primary cilia show abnormal number, morphology, and length in JS models, indicating disrupted cilium organization.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome-derived induced pluripotent stem cells show altered neuronal differentiation in vitro.
evidence.snippet: In addition, analysis of primary cilium count and morphology showed notable ciliary defects in all differentiating JS patient-derived iPSCs compared to controls.

evidence.reference_title: Deficiency of the minor spliceosome component U4atac snRNA secondarily results in ciliary defects in human and zebrafish.
evidence.snippet: Deficiency of the minor spliceosome component U4atac snRNA secondarily results in ciliary defects in human and zebrafish.

evidence.reference_title: Shared and unique consequences of Joubert Syndrome gene dysfunction on the zebrafish central nervous system.
evidence.snippet: We found that JBTS mutants have altered primary cilia throughout the brain.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular treatment options for patients carrying KIAA0586/TALPID3 variants.
evidence.snippet: The patient-derived fibroblasts exhibited reduced primary cilia length and altered distribution of PCM1.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome-derived induced pluripotent stem cells show altered neuronal differentiation in vitro.
evidence.snippet: Joubert syndrome (JS) is a recessively inherited congenital ataxia characterized by hypotonia, psychomotor delay, abnormal ocular movements, intellectual disability, and a peculiar cerebellar and brainstem malformation, the "molar tooth sign."

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome a rare entity and role of radiology: A case report.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features can be noticed shortly after birth that includes hypotonia episodic tachypnea and apnea that may be followed by developmental delays and speech apraxia.
HP:0002104 | Apnea | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome: Molecular basis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Other characteristic features are hypotonia with lateral ataxia, intellectual disability/mental retardation, oculomotor apraxia, retinal dystrophy, abnormalities in the respiratory system, renal cysts, hepatic fibrosis, and skeletal changes.
HP:0001251 | Ataxia | Very frequent (99-80%)
B9D1 | B9 domain containing 1 | hgnc:24123 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in
B9D2 | B9 domain containing 2 | hgnc:28636 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

nodes.description: TMEM67 variants confer the highest risk of liver fibrosis among JS genotypes. Mechanistically, B9D2 — a TMEM67 partner at the ciliary transition zone — also acts before ciliogenesis to mature and maintain epithelial tight junctions, ensuring biliary lumen formation. Loss of B9D2 function disrupts cholangiocyte tight junctions and biliary lumen formation, producing the ductal plate malformation that underlies congenital hepatic fibrosis and portal hypertension in JS.
nodes.id: Biliary ductal plate malformation via ciliary tight junction defect

evidence.reference_title: New functions of B9D2 in tight junctions and epithelial polarity.
evidence.snippet: Our work demonstrates that before ciliogenesis occurs, B9D2 is crucial for the maturation and maintenance of tight junctions ensuring epithelial barrier tightness and appropriate biliary lumen formation.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlates in Joubert syndrome: A review.
evidence.snippet: For instance, JS individuals harboring pathogenic variants in TMEM67 have a significantly higher risk of liver fibrosis, while pathogenic variants in NPHP1, RPGRIP1L, and TMEM237 are frequently associated to JS with renal involvement, requiring a closer monitoring of liver parameters, or renal functioning.

nodes.description: The same mid-hindbrain malformation that produces the molar tooth sign disrupts brainstem nuclei controlling respiratory rhythm and ocular motor pathways, manifesting in neonates as episodic tachypnea and apnea and as oculomotor apraxia and other abnormal eye movements.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome a rare entity and role of radiology: A case report.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features can be noticed shortly after birth that includes hypotonia episodic tachypnea and apnea that may be followed by developmental delays and speech apraxia.

evidence.reference_title: New insights into CC2D2A-related Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: cohort of patients with Joubert syndrome (JS) carrying pathogenic variants on one of the most frequent causative genes, CC2D2A.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic characteristics of 36 children with Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RPGRIP1l: c.1351-11A > G; CEP120: c.214 C > T(p.Arg72Cys).

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlates in Joubert syndrome: A review.
evidence.snippet: On the other hand, individuals with causal variants in the CEP290 or AHI1 need a closer surveillance for retinal dystrophy and, in case of CEP290, also for chronic kidney disease.

evidence.reference_title: A case report of Joubert syndrome with renal involvement and seizures in a neonate.
evidence.snippet: Genome sequencing revealed a mutation involving the CEP290 gene that ultimately confirmed the diagnosis of Joubert syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing and RNA analysis identify two novel CPLANE1 variants causing Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We identified two novel heterozygous variants of CPLANE1 in the proband first, including c.4459del (frameshift variant) and c.7534-14G > A (intronic variant).

evidence.reference_title: Uncertain significance and molecular insights of CPLANE1 variants in prenatal diagnosis of Joubert syndrome: a case report.
evidence.snippet: revealing a pair of compound heterozygous CPLANE1 variants

evidence.reference_title: Non-classic splicing mutation in the CPLANE1 (C5orf42) gene cause Joubert syndrome in a fetus with severe craniocerebral dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The whole exome sequencing identified a novel compound heterozygous variation in the CPLANE1 gene related with Joubert syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic spectrum from a prototype of ciliopathy: Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most common variants were in the CPLANE1, CEP290, and TMEM67 genes, and other causative genes were AHI1, ARMC9, CEP41, CSPP1, HYLS1, KATNIP, KIAA0586, KIF7, RPGRIP1L, including some previously unreported variants in these genes.

evidence.reference_title: A CSPP1 variant associated with metabolic dysfunction in Joubert syndrome: a case report.
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic CSPP1 variants account for approximately 3% of Joubert syndrome cases.

nodes.description: Caffeine citrate to ameliorate central apneic episodes in neonates with Joubert syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Anesthetic management in Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: suggest consideration of the use of caffeine to ameliorate the apneic episodes seen in early infancy in this syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Genetics Review: Joubert Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Classic defects include hypoplasia of the cerebellar vermis, thickened cerebellar peduncles, and deepened interpeduncular fossa, which is regarded as a "molar tooth" sign.

nodes.description: Causative JS genes encode proteins required for primary cilium structure and assembly, disrupting cilium biogenesis in vivo.
nodes.id: Ciliary gene mutations disrupt primary cilium assembly

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome-derived induced pluripotent stem cells show altered neuronal differentiation in vitro.
evidence.snippet: Over 40 causative genes have been reported, all encoding for proteins implicated in the structure or functioning of the primary cilium, a subcellular organelle widely present in embryonic and adult tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Ciliopathy-related B9 protein complex regulates ciliary axonemal microtubule posttranslational modifications and initiation of ciliogenesis.
evidence.snippet: B9 proteins localized to centrioles prior to ciliogenesis, where they facilitated the initiation of ciliogenesis.

nodes.description: The ciliary transition zone (TZ) is a gatekeeper subdomain that controls passage of signaling proteins into and out of the cilium. The TMEM67/MKS module (TMEM67, MKS1, B9D1, B9D2, CC2D2A) and the NPHP module are anchored at the TZ; pathogenic variants in JS genes encoding TZ components reduce TZ gating, anchoring of TMEM67, and posttranslational tubulin modifications.

evidence.reference_title: The ciliary transition zone protein TMEM218 synergistically interacts with the NPHP module and its reduced dosage leads to a wide range of syndromic ciliopathies.
evidence.snippet: We identified biallelic missense and nonsense mutations in the gene encoding the transmembrane protein TMEM218 in unrelated patients with features related to Bardet-Biedl, Joubert and Meckel-Gruber syndrome (MKS) and characterized TMEM218 as a major component of the ciliary TZ module.

evidence.reference_title: Ciliopathy-related B9 protein complex regulates ciliary axonemal microtubule posttranslational modifications and initiation of ciliogenesis.
evidence.snippet: The B9 complex interacted with and anchored TMEM67 to the TZ membrane.

nodes.description: Hedgehog signal transduction depends on regulated trafficking of Smoothened and other Hh components through the primary cilium. Disrupted ciliary structure or transition zone gating in JS reduces Hh signaling, perturbing Hh-dependent neural patterning and limb development.

evidence.reference_title: Togaram1 is expressed in the neural tube and its absence causes neural tube closure defects.
evidence.snippet: We show that Togaram1 is expressed in the neural tube and Togaram1 knockout mice have abnormal cilia, reduced sonic hedgehog (Shh) signaling, abnormal neural tube patterning, and display neural tube closure defects.

nodes.description: AHI1 (Jouberin) participates in canonical Wnt signal transduction. In Ahi1-mutant mouse cerebellum, Wnt reporter activity is reduced at the site of cerebellar hemisphere fusion, producing a vermis-midline fusion defect that recapitulates the human cerebellar vermis hypoplasia of JS. The defect is partially rescued by lithium, a Wnt pathway agonist.
nodes.id: Defective canonical Wnt signaling at the cerebellar midline

evidence.reference_title: Defective Wnt-dependent cerebellar midline fusion in a mouse model of Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Our findings implicate a defect in Wnt signaling in the cerebellar midline phenotype seen in Joubert syndrome that can be overcome with Wnt stimulation.

evidence.reference_title: Defective Wnt-dependent cerebellar midline fusion in a mouse model of Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These mice show cerebellar hypoplasia with a vermis-midline fusion defect early in development.

nodes.description: A ciliary tip module composed of CEP104, CSPP1, TOGARAM1, ARMC9 and CCDC66 controls the very slow processive growth of axonemal microtubules. Loss-of-function variants in module members destabilize this assembly and disrupt axonemal microtubule dynamics that are required to build and maintain the primary cilium.

evidence.reference_title: A network of interacting ciliary tip proteins with opposing activities imparts slow and processive microtubule growth.
evidence.snippet: Here we reconstituted in vitro the individual and collective activities of the ciliary tip module proteins CEP104, CSPP1, TOGARAM1, ARMC9 and CCDC66, which interact with each other and with microtubules and, when mutated in humans, cause ciliopathies such as Joubert syndrome.

nodes.description: Disrupted ciliary signaling perturbs morphogenesis of the midbrain-hindbrain boundary, producing the characteristic JS malformation: hypoplasia of the cerebellar vermis, thickened/elongated superior cerebellar peduncles, and a deepened interpeduncular fossa.

evidence.reference_title: Genetics Review: Joubert Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Classic defects include hypoplasia of the cerebellar vermis, thickened cerebellar peduncles, and deepened interpeduncular fossa, which is regarded as a "molar tooth" sign.

nodes.description: A defining feature of JS is failure of axons to cross the midline at the superior cerebellar peduncles, the central pontine tracts, and the corticospinal tracts. This points to a defective axon-guidance program at the mid-hindbrain junction and contributes directly to the molar tooth sign and the cerebellar/motor phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome and related disorders, prenatal diagnosis with ultrasound and magnetic resonance imaging.
evidence.snippet: JSRD is characterized by lack of decussation of the superior cerebellar peduncles, central pontine tracts and corticospinal tracts suggesting defective axon guidance.

evidence.snippet: Other characteristic features are hypotonia with lateral ataxia, intellectual disability/mental retardation, oculomotor apraxia, retinal dystrophy, abnormalities in the respiratory system, renal cysts, hepatic fibrosis, and skeletal changes.

nodes.description: KIAA0586/Talpid3 is a basal-body protein required for primary cilium assembly. Talpid3 mutant mice show reduced proliferation in the dentate gyrus, a disrupted glial scaffold, and mis-localized progenitors in the granule cell layer — defective hippocampal neurogenesis that links ciliary dysfunction to learning and memory deficits seen in JS patients.

evidence.reference_title: Hippocampals neurogenesis is impaired in mice with a deletion in the coiled coil domain of Talpid3-implications for Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: At early postnatal stages, the Talpid3 mutants exhibit a reduction in proliferation in the dentate gyrus and a disrupted glial scaffold.

evidence.reference_title: Hippocampals neurogenesis is impaired in mice with a deletion in the coiled coil domain of Talpid3-implications for Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Our findings suggest a link between the hippocampal defects and the learning/memory deficits seen in JS patients.

evidence.snippet: Two recurrent variants (MKS1 c.1476T>G and KIAA0586 c.428delG), have been detected in homozygosity in unaffected individuals, suggesting they could act as hypomorphic variants.

nodes.description: Biallelic (or hemizygous, for X-linked OFD1) pathogenic variants in over 40 ciliary genes initiate Joubert syndrome. The encoded proteins localize to distinct primary cilium subcompartments — basal body, transition zone, axoneme/ciliary tip, and intraflagellar transport machinery — and converge on disrupted primary cilium structure and function.

evidence.reference_title: Joubert syndrome-derived induced pluripotent stem cells show altered neuronal differentiation in vitro.
evidence.snippet: Over 40 causative genes have been reported, all encoding for proteins implicated in the structure or functioning of the primary cilium, a subcellular organelle widely present in embryonic and adult tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic spectrum from a prototype of ciliopathy: Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most common variants were in the CPLANE1, CEP290, and TMEM67 genes, and other causative genes were AHI1, ARMC9, CEP41, CSPP1, HYLS1, KATNIP, KIAA0586, KIF7, RPGRIP1L, including some previously unreported variants in these genes.

nodes.description: Photoreceptor outer segments are highly modified primary cilia. Pathogenic JS variants affecting transition zone gating (CEP290) or its interactors (NPHP5/IQCB1) produce aberrantly elongated ciliary axonemes, impaired outer segment development, and mislocalization of visual pigments to photoreceptor cell soma — the cellular basis of JS-associated retinal dystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: In vitro modeling and rescue of ciliopathy associated with IQCB1/NPHP5 mutations using patient-derived cells.
evidence.snippet: Organoids revealed impaired development of outer segment structures, which are modified primary cilia, and mislocalization of visual pigments to photoreceptor cell soma.

evidence.reference_title: In vitro modeling and rescue of ciliopathy associated with IQCB1/NPHP5 mutations using patient-derived cells.
evidence.snippet: All patient-derived cells showed reduced levels of CEP290 protein, a critical cilia transition zone component interacting with NPHP5, providing a plausible mechanism for aberrant ciliary gating and cargo transport.

evidence.reference_title: Any modality of renal replacement therapy can be a treatment option for Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Peritoneal dialysis (PD) was introduced in seven cases, with a median treatment duration of 5.4 (3.4-10.7) years.

nodes.description: Renal tubular epithelial cells require functional primary cilia for homeostasis. Variants in JS genes encoding transition zone (CEP290) and NPHP-module components disrupt tubular ciliary signaling and drive nephronophthisis-type tubulointerstitial disease and renal cyst formation. CEP290 variants in particular confer chronic kidney disease risk that warrants surveillance.

evidence.snippet: was referred to the Sleep Unit because spells of apnea while sleeping.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic spectrum from a prototype of ciliopathy: Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most common variants were in the CPLANE1, CEP290, and TMEM67 genes, and other causative genes were AHI1, ARMC9, CEP41, CSPP1, HYLS1, KATNIP, KIAA0586, KIF7, RPGRIP1L, including some previously unreported variants in these genes.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlates in Joubert syndrome: A review.
evidence.snippet: For instance, JS individuals harboring pathogenic variants in TMEM67 have a significantly higher risk of liver fibrosis, while pathogenic variants in NPHP1, RPGRIP1L, and TMEM237 are frequently associated to JS with renal involvement, requiring a closer monitoring of liver parameters, or renal functioning.

evidence.reference_title: Novel ocular observations in a child with Joubert syndrome type 6 due to pathogenic variant in TMEM67 gene.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of autosomal recessive Joubert syndrome type 6 due to homozygous pathogenic variant c.725A > G p. (Asn242Ser) in TMEM67 gene was confirmed by whole exome sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: Compound heterozygous TMEM67 biallelic variants including a novel frameshift mutation in two Filipino adolescent siblings with Joubert syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Whole Exome Sequencing (WES), performed via buccal swab, showed biallelic pathogenic variants at NM_153704.6:c.2086 C > T (NP_714915.3:p.Leu696Phe) and NM_153704.6:c.431del (NP_714915.3:p.Leu144CysfsTer19) in TMEM67, which are associated with Joubert Syndrome 6 (OMIM:610688) in a compound heterozygous state.
